steps.title: Use the charge benchmark
steps.content: The experiment showed that the European peacock butterfly's maximum positive charge is high enough to facilitate electrostatic pollen transfer. That makes its charge a working benchmark for identifying another pollinator likely to show the same capacity.

steps.title: Reject graph misreadings
steps.content: A claims an opposite tendency, B overstates what can be concluded about the minimum charge, and D reverses the logic by saying greater charges should prevent the capacity. The graph supports the pollinator with a higher maximum positive charge also demonstrating it. The answer is C.
